rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GLEN RAY TODD
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) The executor made preliminary distribution of $140,000 to herself individually without prior court approval. No proof that the unlawfully-distributed funds have been returned the estate has been filed. 2) The petition requests compensation for the executor's attorney's extraordinary services in the total amount of $5,855.50. Although the attorney hourly billed $11,739.50 for ordinary services, the attorney's statutory compensation for such ordinary services is capped at $7,456.87. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOSE ALFREDO ZERMENO II
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The Court to determine if the report of status of administration should be approved now. 1) The Final I&A filed on 2/24/23 shows three vehicles, all appraised by the administrator, not by the probate referee. [Probate Code Section 8902(b).] 2) The date notice (to creditors) was mailed to the FTB must be set forth. [LRSF 14.35 I.2.] 3) If an accounting will be filed, the first two-year accounting ending 1/3/21 is long overdue. [LRSF 14.34 A.] This was raised at the last hearing on this petition on 7/18/22. 4) Administrator requested six more months to administer the estate when he verified this petition on 1/19/22; that would have extended estate administration to July 2022. PARTIES APPEARING MUST CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME. PLEASE HAVE YOUR CASE NUMBER AND NAME READY FOR THE CLERK. Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: Meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF CLARENCE JOSEPH SUGHROUE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/21/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: 1. The second "Declaration" (filed on 4/3/23) was an AMENDMENT and must be (re)filed as such. [See CRC 7.3 for definitions of pleadings.] 2. An amended pleading or an amendment to a pleading requires the same notice of hearing as the pleading it amends. [CRC 7.53(a).] 3. Not all of the first "Declaration" filed on 3/6/23 has been reviewed at this time; however, page 16 of 17 of the Disbursements schedule attached to the original petition and page 17 of 17 of the "corrected" (amended) final account attached to this "Declaration" clearly show "Automobile Expenses"... Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 6/8/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Issues should be addressed simply in a supplement or an amendment to a petition (or a completely amended petition) that stands on its own, not in "response" to an examiner. As noted in the prior tentative ruling, wherein specific code sections and rules of court or local rules were cited, laws and rules govern what must be disclosed or filed, not an examiner's "requests" for an accounting subsequent to the date of death or bank statements or full descriptions of disbursements, etc.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF IGNACIO JIMENEZ PLASCENCIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/5/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) A "declaration" by the attorney was filed on 3/6/23. No courtesy copy of it has been received yet by the assigned examiner, as required by LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B. and C. (2) A Court-appointed fiduciary must sign and verify his own pleadings, including any supplement or amendment thereto. (3) The revision to the petition caption is an amendment. An amendment requires notice. [CRC 7.53(a).] Courtesy copies of (1) the amendment to the petition, signed and verified by the executor, and (2) the proof of service of notice of the petition and the amendment to the petition, which notice also shows that procedures for appearing remotely were attached to it, must be received by the assigned Examiner by 5/22/23 to avoid a continuance. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. NB: When papers are not filed electronically, each paper presented for filing must contain two prepunched normal-sized holes, centered 21/2 inches apart and 5/8 inch from the top of the paper. [CRC 2.115.] This includes proposed orders.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF EDWARD GARLAND NOBLE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 05/22/2023 (no earlier date available), as the following issues remain unaddressed: 1) 7% interest must be paid on the $3,000 cash gift to Catherine Noble, as required by Probate Code section 12003. The petition must be amended to allege a specific amount of interest payable as of a date certain, together with a daily rate thereafter. 2) The Schwab account statement attached to the petition does not show 770 shares of Bank of America common stock inventoried and appraised. If the shares have been sold, the petition must be amended to provide the information required by CRC 7.550(b)(2) & (3), remove the shares from the assets on hand, and modify the schedule of proposed distribution. Note: Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THOMAS & JOAN GILL LIVING TRUST DATED SEPTEMBER 10, 2002
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Petition For Orders: 1)Compelling Trustee To Produce Trust Records; 2) Suspending Trustee And Appointing Temporary Successor Trustee 3) Removing Trustee And Appointing Successor Trustee 4) Surcharging Trustee 5) Denying Trustee Compensation; And 6) Awarding Attorney'S Fees And Costs To Petitioners
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required, although a copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(D)(6) was not attached to the DE-120 form on file. The Court is to take a first look at the contested pleadings and determine how best to proceed. No guardian ad litem has been appointed for Joan Gill, the surviving trustor and beneficiary alleged to lack legal capacity. It is unclear whether Joan Gill has a power of attorney, and if so, whether the attorney-in-fact has authority to exercise Joan Gill's rights and powers granted by the trust (including the rights and powers to modify or revoke the Survivor's Trust and to exercise a limited power of appointment over the Family or Marital Trust). PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.
